note: Antibiotics do not treat viral colds, flu or COVID-19.

title: Influenza (flu)
organisation: Australian Government Department of Health, Disability and Ageing
note: Stay home, distance, ventilation, masks and respiratory hygiene.

title: Influenza (flu) vaccine
organisation: Australian Government Department of Health, Disability and Ageing
note: 2026 vaccine products and NIP-funded groups.

title: Influenza (flu)
note: Annual vaccination recommendations, age groups and dosing exceptions.

title: Proposed amendments to the Poisons Standard — pseudoephedrine
note: Confirms pseudoephedrine preparations remain Schedule 3 or Schedule 4 depending on formulation.

title: FDA proposes ending use of oral phenylephrine as an OTC decongestant
note: FDA review concluded oral phenylephrine is not effective for nasal congestion; proposal does not apply to nasal phenylephrine.

title: Zinc for prevention and treatment of the common cold
note: Possible shorter duration, low certainty and more non-serious adverse effects.

title: Over-the-counter medications for acute cough
note: No good evidence for or against common OTC cough preparations.

title: Honey for acute cough in children
note: Low-to-moderate certainty; possible short-term cough/sleep benefit; never under 12 months.

title: Heated, humidified air for the common cold
note: Evidence does not establish meaningful benefit and steam carries burn risk.

title: Nasal saline irrigation for acute upper airway infection symptoms
note: Possible symptom benefit, but small trials and high risk of bias.

title: Oral antihistamine-decongestant-analgesic combinations for the common cold
note: Small overall benefit in adults and older children, no benefit in younger children, some increased side effects.

title: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs for the common cold
note: Helps pain-related symptoms, not clearly cough or runny nose.

title: Echinacea for preventing and treating the common cold
note: No clear preventive benefit and inconsistent treatment results across preparations.

title: Colds, flu, and complementary health approaches
organisation: US National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health
note: Evidence for vitamin C, echinacea, garlic and similar products is conflicting, inadequate or mostly negative.

title: Treatment of flu
organisation: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
note: Antivirals work best within 48 hours and are particularly important for severe or high-risk illness.

title: How to safely rinse sinuses
organisation: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
note: Use distilled, sterile, or boiled and cooled water; never straight tap water.

title: Is rinsing your sinuses with neti pots safe?
note: Safe water, device cleaning and correct technique.

title: Humidifiers: ease skin, breathing symptoms
note: Aim for 30–50% indoor humidity and clean devices to prevent mould/bacterial exposure.
